summary.intent: Fix GitHub issues and discussions header icons that were rendering as plain black squares after being added to the UI.
summary.outcome: Fixed both SVG mask files by adding fill-rule="evenodd" to their path elements, and rebuilt the Tailwind CSS output (unminified). Both changes were committed together.

repo: Icons use CSS mask-image technique with background-color: currentColor — SVGs must be designed as masks, not colored icons.
style.css must be stored unminified so diffs merge cleanly (per CLAUDE.md).
GitHub Octicon SVG paths require fill-rule="evenodd" to render correctly as masks; without it, compound paths fill solid instead of punching holes.

code.finding: Base .icon class uses CSS mask-image with background-color: currentColor for icon rendering.
Added fill-rule="evenodd" to path elements so the ring/outline paths render with holes rather than as solid filled shapes.
Same fill-rule="evenodd" fix required as mask-github-issue.svg.

workflow: When CSS mask icons render as solid black rectangles, the SVG likely has compound paths missing fill-rule="evenodd".
Tailwind CSS rebuild can incidentally fix rendering issues by regenerating the stylesheet — but underlying SVG correctness should still be fixed explicitly.

friction: Multiple grep attempts were needed to locate the base .icon class definition — it was generated by Tailwind rather than in a hand-authored CSS file, making it harder to find.
The icons appeared to fix after CSS rebuild, creating ambiguity about whether the SVG fill-rule fix was actually necessary.
